text: This case presentation from the Cleveland Clinic demonstrates single-part laparoscopic restorative proctocolectomy with ileal pouch anal anastomosis through the left lower quadrant ileostomy site in a three-stage fashion. This thirty-nine-year-old female presented to the clinic with a medically refractory ulcerative colitis. Since patient has a congenital atrophy in her right upper extremity, a right ileostomy site was deemed unmanageable for her. and a decision was made to use the left lower quadrant for the ileostomy. Due to high steroid use and medically refractory nature of the disease, patient was first taken to the operating room for a single incisional laparoscopic total colectomy with creation of a left-sided ileostomy. Patient was positioned in the modified lithotomy. A disc shape previously marked skin from the left lower quadrant was excised and abdomen was entered in a muscle splitting fashion. One twelve and two five millimeter trackers was introduced through the jet platform. Dissection was started from the right colon and initially ileocolic pedicle was identified and ligated. Medial to lateral dissection was performed. Duodenum was identified and preserved. The ascending colon completely mobilized and clockwise right to left total colectomy was performed. The hepatocollic ligament is then taken down and transverse colon is mobilized by dividing the mesocolon and the great romantum. After the splenic flexure was taken down, the left branch of the middle collic artery was ligated. Inferior mesenteric artery was left intact and recto-sigmoid junction was stapled. Ultimately, the colon was exteriorized from the ileostomy site. Ileostomy was matured and rectal stem was left in pelvis with no implantation. The patient's postoperative recovery was uneventful.

Six months post-colectomy, the surgeon performs a circumferential incision around the stoma site in the left lower quadrant. The loop ileostomy is released, temporarily sealed with a trio-viral stick, and returned to the abdomen. Pneumoperitoneum is established using a single port access device.

The surgeon ligates the inferior mesenteric artery at the level of the superior artery and dissects through the avascular plane posterior to the mesorectum, continuing down to the levator muscles. Dissection is carried out bilaterally upon reaching the levator muscles, followed by anterior mobilization. After achieving complete mobilization, a digital rectal exam confirms the dissection level. The laparoscopic GIA-XVI stapler is fired twice to staple the rectum.

A 15 to 20 cm ileal pouch is created using a stapling device. A leak test is performed, showing no leaks. A purse-string suture is placed with 2-0 Prolene, and an EEA-28 stapler is inserted into the pouch. Under laparoscopic view, an intracorporeal anastomosis is created, followed by a pouchoscopy. A loop of bowel proximal to the pouch is exteriorized, and the ileostomy is matured.
The patient's postoperative recovery was uneventful. This case demonstrates the feasibility and safety of single-port surgery in IPA patients using the left lower quadrant ileostomacite.
